pattern: Collecting the tree's values into a hash set turns the question into a one-pass complement lookup: a pair summing to the target exists when some value's partner has already been seen.
hints: You are only asked whether a pair exists, not to find it, so any traversal that visits every value works as the source of candidates.
For each value v, its partner must equal target minus v; remembering values you have already visited lets you check that in constant time.
Walk the whole tree once, and before storing each value check whether its complement is already in the set, returning true the moment one matches.

edge_inputs.description: single node cannot form a pair
single node value doubled is not a pair (one element)
two nodes form the only pair
k equals a value but needs distinct nodes

stress.description: random BST with a target chosen so a real pair sometimes exists
stress.note: distinct sampled values keep the BST valid
